title: selaginella robinsonii
intro: Selaginella robinsonii, also known as Robinson's spikemoss, is a species of spikemoss in the family Selaginellaceae. It is a small, creeping plant with needle-like leaves that is native to the tropical regions of Southeast Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Selaginella robinsonii include reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and improving digestion, and it is often prepared as a tea or dried into capsules for herbal use.
